2604.24083.title: The Kerimov-Alekberli Model: An Information-Geometric Framework for Real-Time System Stability
2604.24083.paper_summary: This study introduces the Kerimov-Alekberli model, a novel information-geometric framework that redefines AI safety by formally linking non-equilibrium thermodynamics to stochastic control for the ethical alignment of autonomous systems. By establishing a formal isomorphism between non-equilibrium thermodynamics and stochastic control, we define systemic anomalies as deviations from a Riemannian manifold. The model utilizes the Kullback-Leibler divergence as the primary metric, governed by a dynamic threshold derived from the Fisher Information Metric. We further ground this framework in the Landauer Principle, proving that adversarial perturbations perform measurable physical work by increasing the system's informational entropy. Validation on the NSL-KDD dataset and unmanned aerial vehicle trajectory simulations demonstrated that our model achieves effective real-time detection via the FPT trigger, with strong performance metrics (e.g., high accuracy and low FPR) on benchmark datasets. This study provides a rigorous physical foundation for AI safety, transitioning from heuristic, rule-based ethical frameworks to a thermodynamics-based stability paradigm by grounding ethical violations in quantifiable physical work and entropic information.
2604.24083.paper_summary_zh: 這項研究介紹了Kerimov-Alekberli模型，一個新穎的信息幾何框架，通過將非平衡熱力學與隨機控制正式聯繫起來，重新定義了AI安全性，以實現自主系統的倫理對齊。<lb/>通過在非平衡熱力學和隨機控制之間建立正式同構，我們將系統異常定義為與黎曼流形的偏差。<lb/>該模型利用Kullback-Leibler散度作為主要度量，受Fisher信息度量導出的動態閾值控制。<lb/>我們進一步將這一框架建立在Landauer原則上，證明對抗擾動通過增加系統的信息熵來執行可測量的物理工作。<lb/>在NSL-KDD數據集和無人機軌跡模擬上的驗證顯示，我們的模型通過FPT觸發器實現了有效的實時檢測，在基準數據集上具有強大的性能指標（例如，高準確率和低FPR）。<lb/>這項研究為AI安全性提供了一個嚴謹的物理基礎，從啟發式、基於規則的倫理框架轉變為基於熱力學的穩定性範式，通過將倫理違規根植於可量化的物理工作和熵信息中。
2604.24083.author: Hikmat Karimov et.al.
